review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls relevant to SOC2 CC7.1. However, the function for network policy monitoring needed a more descriptive name to clarify its purpose of monitoring changes.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly describe the specific check being performed, such as monitoring changes rather than generic monitoring.
title: CC7.1 To meet its objectives, the entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to identify (1) changes to configurations that result in the introduction of new vulnerabilities, and (2) susceptibilities to newly discovered vulnerabilities
step1_reasoning: The control requires detection and monitoring of configuration changes and vulnerabilities. In Kubernetes, this can be addressed through audit logging for configuration changes, admission controllers for vulnerability scanning on resource creation, image scanning for vulnerabilities, and monitoring network policies for unauthorized changes.
